01.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Epiplastron.
01.Contour: The top edge is a smooth original curve, while the inner edges are jagged sutures.
01.Pattern: It features a curved groove line sweeping downwards to the right.

02.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Epiplastron.
02.Contour: The top edge is a smooth original curve, while the inner edges are jagged sutures.
02.Pattern: It features a curved groove line sweeping downwards to the left.

03.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hyoplastron.
03.Contour: The right edge is a straight vertical midline suture, while the left edge is a curved bridge suture. The top inner corner has a notch to fit the central diamond bone.
03.Pattern: Characterized by horizontal texture, often showing two parallel groove lines.

04.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hyoplastron.
04.Contour: The left edge is a straight vertical midline suture, while the right edge is a curved bridge suture. The top inner corner has a notch to fit the central diamond bone.
04.Pattern: Characterized by horizontal texture, often showing two parallel groove lines.

05.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Hypoplastron.
05.Contour: The right edge is a straight vertical suture. The top edge is horizontal, and the bottom edge is irregular where it meets the tail bone.
05.Pattern: Features a single horizontal groove line that forms a slightly backward-convex arc.

06.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Hypoplastron.
06.Contour: The left edge is a straight vertical suture. The top edge is horizontal, and the bottom edge is irregular where it meets the tail bone.
06.Pattern: Features a single horizontal groove line that forms a slightly backward-convex arc.

07.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Left Xiphiplastron.
07.Contour: The top edge is jagged, and the bottom tip is usually concave or pointed.
07.Pattern: Features a groove line sloping steeply downwards to the left.

08.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Right Xiphiplastron.
08.Contour: The top edge is jagged, and the bottom tip is usually concave or pointed.
08.Pattern: Features a groove line sloping steeply downwards to the right.

09.prefix: A close-up photo of an ancient Chinese turtle plastron fragment, specifically the Entoplastron.
09.Contour: The fragment has a rhomboid or diamond-shaped outline formed by jagged suture lines.
09.Pattern: The surface features a cross-shaped (+) intersection of grooves.
